text:
Once upon an English afternoon, there were three ducks.

There was old Winston. Winston was wise; he lived eighty winters. In his long life he collected many adventures. One thing he loved most was his garden. In it he grew carrots.

There was young Winston. Young Winston loved the wind. Sometimes wind became a friend. Sometimes wind became a stranger. Today, wind was stranger.

You see, Winston loved flying. Sometimes he flew high. Sometimes he flew low. Sometimes he dove. He loved it all.

Then there was young Winston's sister. Young Winston adored squirrels. They did little jumps. They climbed little trees. That was fun. That was lively.

But young Winston was shy. Squirrels were scary.

On this fine day young Winston saw his sisters. One loved jumping. One liked trees. One liked squirrels. That was good. That was a good family.

Young Winston loved them all.

They loved him.

That day they jumped together. They climbed
